title: Scale to ≥50 chapters published across ≥2 works/collections
context: With the M1 pipeline validated at ~10 chapters, M2 scales to ≥50 chapters across ≥2 works/collections to produce meaningful, learner-usable read-alongs. Quality bars must be held: acceptance rate ≥85%, pronunciation error rate ≤2/1,000, 0 fidelity defects, audio-quality conformance ≥95%, alignment within tolerance, 100% consent and rights completeness. This is the milestone that makes the open commons meaningfully more accessible for literacy learners. Depends on batch-001.
objective: Scale the pipeline to publish ≥50 chapters upstream across ≥2 works/collections while holding all quality, consent, rights, and labeling bars from M1.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥50 chapters are published upstream and live across ≥2 distinct works/collections.
Pronunciation error rate ≤2 per 1,000 words at QA; 0 uncorrected pronunciation errors merged.
0 text-audio fidelity defects merged (no skipped, added, or substituted words vs. source).
Audio-quality conformance ≥95% of chapters passing automated loudness/peak/noise checks on first pass.
Alignment within tolerance for all published chapters; median onset error at or below the target established in narrate-001.
100% consent completeness (signed consent or synthetic-provenance record for every voice) and 100% per-jurisdiction rights verification; 0 violations; 100% labeled human-narrated or AI-synthesized.
resources: read-aloud-audio-batch-001 (batch-001 run report, acceptance rate baseline)
output: ≥50 chapters published at the confirmed upstream channel across ≥2 works/collections (live URLs / PR / deposit references), updated per-work records, and a batch-002 run report recording acceptance rate, pronunciation error rate, fidelity defect count, audio-quality conformance rate, alignment accuracy, and consent/rights completeness.
